name: Medallion Bank
description_short: Medallion Bank provides point-of-sale consumer financing for RV/boat dealers and home improvement contractors, plus banking-as-a-service partnerships for lenders.
description_long:
Medallion Bank specializes in indirect consumer lending through B2B partnerships rather than direct-to-consumer loans. The company functions as a financing facilitator, providing point-of-sale lending solutions to dealers and contractors who want to offer their customers financing options at the moment of purchase. Their business model centers on partnering with recreation vehicle dealers, boat dealers, home improvement contractors, and strategic partners building lending platforms.

Medallion Bank's core services include financing programs for RV and boat purchases through recreation dealers, home improvement project financing for contractors, and banking-as-a-service (BaaS) solutions for strategic partners seeking lending program support and regulatory compliance assistance. For borrowers, they offer online account management through two platforms depending on loan origination date (my.medallionbank.com for post-November 2024 loans, Accountinfo.com for earlier loans). They explicitly market themselves as serving customers with credit challenges, positioning inclusive lending as a competitive advantage for their dealer partners.

The company distinguishes itself through dedicated, specialized support for each market segment—separate phone lines, account management systems, and even different holiday schedules between recreation and home improvement departments reflect their segmented approach. They emphasize modern technology paired with traditional customer service, and operate extended borrower services hours (Monday-Friday 8am-11pm MT, Saturday 9am-1pm MT) compared to standard business hours. Their advocacy work on Capitol Hill suggests engagement with regulatory and policy issues affecting their lending model.

Medallion Bank is fundamentally a B2B lending enabler, not a direct consumer lender. This positioning means consumers cannot apply directly but must go through their dealer or contractor network. Their profitability depends on volume through partners, and their success requires robust dealer relationships. While their inclusive lending approach is genuinely consumer-friendly, prospective borrowers have no direct control over whether financing will be available—it depends entirely on whether their chosen dealer or contractor partners with Medallion.
services: Point-of-sale RV and boat financing through recreation vehicle dealer partnerships
Home improvement project financing through contractor partnerships
Banking-as-a-service (BaaS) lending program support and regulatory compliance infrastructure
Online loan account management and payment processing (my.medallionbank.com for post-November 2024 loans)
Legacy loan account management (Accountinfo.com for loans funded before November 2024)
Borrower customer service and loan support (8am-11pm MT weekdays, 9am-1pm MT Saturdays)
Dealer and contractor business development and financing solutions consultation
Inclusive lending programs targeting customers with credit challenges or limited credit history
Technology platform partnerships for embedded lending in third-party financing platforms
Dedicated support lines for recreation dealers (866-688-6983) and home improvement contractors (888-833-8570)
pros: Extended customer service hours for borrowers (8am-11pm MT weekdays, 9am-1pm MT Saturdays) beyond typical business hours
Explicitly designed to serve customers with credit challenges through partner dealers, expanding access for subprime borrowers
Dual online account management platforms with transition support for borrowers with loans from different time periods
Specialized support teams for distinct markets (recreation, home improvement) with dedicated phone lines and expertise
Banking-as-a-service partnerships enable regulatory compliance support for lenders building their own platforms
Point-of-sale financing eliminates need for consumers to apply separately—integrated into dealer/contractor purchase process
Transparent holiday schedule and operational hours published clearly for all customer types
cons: No direct consumer application process—financing only available through partner dealers and contractors, limiting borrower choice
Consumers cannot shop rates or terms independently; available only if their specific dealer/contractor uses Medallion
Website provides minimal information about loan terms, rates, eligibility requirements, or APR ranges for consumers
Account management system migration (pre/post-November 2024 split) creates potential confusion for some borrowers
No transparent disclosure of whether subprime lending comes with significantly higher rates or fees

contact.address: 1100 E 6600 S, Salt Lake City, UT 84121

best_for: RV and boat buyers purchasing from dealers partnered with Medallion Bank who have limited credit options elsewhere
Home improvement contractors seeking to offer financing as a value-added service to their customers
Recreation dealers wanting to expand their addressable market by offering credit-inclusive purchasing options
Financial technology companies and strategic partners building lending platforms requiring banking-as-a-service infrastructure
diagnosis: Medallion Bank is best for consumers purchasing RVs, boats, or home improvements through dealers or contractors already partnered with the company—particularly those with limited credit options. The primary caveat is that financing availability depends entirely on whether your specific dealer or contractor uses Medallion; you cannot apply directly or shop rates independently as an individual consumer.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 1100 E 6600 S, Salt Lake City, UT 84121
